activity_coefficient_basis: Parent cement-equivalent activity remains the single demand anchor.
double_counting_guardrail: Selecting the decomposition representation must deactivate make_cement_equivalent direct aggregate methods so aggregate cement energy and process emissions cannot also run.
notes: This row defines the mutual-exclusion boundary for later aggregate and decomposed cement representations.

activity_coefficient_basis: Clinker activity derives from parent cement-equivalent output through the selected clinker-to-cement ratio.
double_counting_guardrail: Calcination CO2 stays with clinker production and must not be duplicated in final cement blending or generic IPPU residual rows.
notes: This child is the physical calcination and clinker-making step.

activity_coefficient_basis: Kiln heat derives from clinker activity through route-specific thermal intensity.
double_counting_guardrail: Host-specific kiln heat must sit inside the decomposed cement branch and must not duplicate the high-temperature industrial heat coefficients folded into make_other_material_products for the same cement output.
notes: This child isolates cement kiln fuel switching and thermal efficiency from the make_other_material_products folded high-temperature industrial heat coefficients.

activity_coefficient_basis: Final grinding and blending activity is one unit per unit of parent cement-equivalent output.
double_counting_guardrail: Final cement-equivalent output must not carry clinker calcination or kiln fuel emissions already assigned to upstream children.
notes: This child closes the product boundary and carries clinker substitution and final electricity use.

activity_coefficient_basis: Captured CO2 is linked from clinker or kiln heat source streams only when a CCS pathway method elects capture.
double_counting_guardrail: CCS pathways may reduce net source emissions through captured-flow accounting but must not create a second abatement credit outside capture transport and storage roles.
notes: This bridge reuses the shared carbon-management roles rather than creating cement-specific transport or storage children.
